claimText: GSAT-7R (CMS-03) was launched aboard ISRO's LVM3 and is designed to eventually settle in a Geostationary Orbit.
evidenceExcerpt: GSAT-7R (CMS-03) was launched aboard ISRO's LVM3 into a Geosynchronous Transfer Orbit (GTO) to eventually settle in Geostationary Orbit.
examUse: Statement-based question on the launch vehicle and final orbit of the GSAT-7R satellite.
distractorHooks: Launched via PSLV instead of LVM3
Settles in Low Earth Orbit (LEO) or Sun-Synchronous Orbit instead of Geostationary Orbit

claimText: GSAT-7R provides multi-band telecommunication services across the Indian Ocean Region up to 2,000 km from India's coastline.
evidenceExcerpt: It provides multi-band telecommunication services across the Indian Ocean Region up to 2,000 km from India's coastline.
examUse: Statement-based question testing the geographical coverage and range of the satellite's services.
distractorHooks: Range is limited to 500 km from the coastline

claimText: GSAT-7R has a 15-year lifespan and replaces the GSAT-7/INSAT-4F Rukmini satellite.
evidenceExcerpt: It has a 15-year lifespan and replaces the GSAT-7/INSAT-4F Rukmini satellite.
examUse: Correct-pair or statement-based question identifying the predecessor satellite that GSAT-7R is replacing.
distractorHooks: Replaces the RISAT series of satellites

claimText: The primary purpose of GSAT-7R is to strengthen maritime domain awareness and secure communication links specifically for the Indian Navy.
evidenceExcerpt: Its primary purpose is to strengthen maritime domain awareness and secure communication links for the Indian Navy.
examUse: Statement-based question on the specific armed force benefiting from the GSAT-7R satellite.
distractorHooks: Dedicated exclusively to the Indian Air Force
